tldr: Switching from GitHub Actions to CircleCI usually means trading a tightly integrated CI for one with stronger orchestration features like parallelism, test splitting, and orbs. The pipeline YAML must be rewritten and secrets rotated.
why_switch: Orbs ecosystem for reusable CI logic
Better support for resource class tuning
Mature self-hosted runner story for regulated workloads
what_to_export: OIDC trust configuration to cloud providers
Status check names referenced by branch protection
export_method: All GitHub Actions workflow definitions are already in the repo under .github/workflows. Treat the migration as a rewrite, not a dump. List every secret and variable from the repository and organization settings — values cannot be exported, only the names. Note which workflows use OIDC to assume cloud roles, since the trust policy will need a new issuer. Capture branch protection rules that reference status check names so you can remap them to the new CircleCI check names. Inventory composite actions and reusable workflows your pipelines call.
import_method: Add a .circleci/config.yml at the repo root and translate workflows job by job. Pick resource classes that match your previous runner sizing. Recreate secrets as CircleCI project or context variables; for shared org-level values, use Contexts. Replace marketplace actions with equivalent orbs or inline shell steps. For cloud auth, switch OIDC trust policies to CircleCI's issuer. Update branch protection in GitHub to require the new CircleCI status checks, then disable the old workflows by deleting or commenting out the .github/workflows files. Run both CIs in parallel for a few merges before fully cutting over.

concept_mapping.note: Re-create trust policy for new issuer

effort: 1-3 weeks depending on number of workflows, custom actions, and OIDC integrations
gotchas: Branch protection rules tied to old check names will block merges until updated
OIDC trust policies must be re-issued; do not reuse the old role conditions
Many GitHub Actions have no 1:1 orb — budget time to write shell steps
Caching semantics differ; cache keys may need restructuring
Per-credit billing requires modeling actual workloads to forecast spend

email.subject: Plan to wind down GitHub Actions usage
email.body:
Hi GitHub team,

We are moving our CI/CD pipelines to a different provider over the next 14 days. We do not need to close our GitHub account, only stop running Actions workloads on the paid plan. Could you confirm how to remove paid Actions minutes from the next invoice, and whether any reserved or prepaid minutes will be lost or refunded?

We would also like to confirm the data retention timeline for build logs and artifacts after we stop running workflows, in case we need a final pull. If there is a notice period, billing cutoff date, or specific setting we need to toggle to avoid the next charge, please share it.

Thanks for the help getting this scheduled cleanly.

Your customer
